title: Agent searches through abstraction layer
story: As an iSDLC agent, I want to search the codebase through a unified abstraction layer so that I get the best available search results without knowing which backend is active.
acceptance_criteria: Agent calls search() with intent and modality hint
Search router selects the best available backend
Results are returned in a uniform format regardless of backend
Agent code does not reference specific search tools (Grep, ast-grep, etc.)

title: Search tools detected during project setup
story: As an iSDLC user, I want the framework to automatically detect available search tools during project setup so that I get optimal search performance without manual configuration.
acceptance_criteria: Framework scans system for search tools during isdlc init
Project file count is assessed for scale tier classification
User is informed of detected tools and recommendations
Existing MCP server configurations are preserved

title: Missing search tools installed with consent
story: As an iSDLC user, I want the framework to offer to install recommended search tools that are missing from my system so that I get enhanced search without manual installation, while remaining informed and in control.
acceptance_criteria: Framework presents recommended tools with explanation
User can accept all, select specific tools, or decline
Installation failures are reported clearly
Failed installations do not block project setup

title: Graceful fallback when enhanced search unavailable
story: As an iSDLC user, I want the framework to fall back to standard search when enhanced backends are unavailable so that my workflow is never blocked, while being informed about reduced precision.
acceptance_criteria: Backend health is checked before routing
Unhealthy backends fall back to next in priority order
User notified once per session on degradation
Workflow continues without interruption

title: AST-aware structural search for code patterns
story: As an iSDLC agent, I want to search for code structures (function definitions, class hierarchies, decorator patterns) so that I can find relevant code by its syntactic structure rather than text matching alone.
acceptance_criteria: Structural queries route to ast-grep when available
Results include AST context (node type, parent scope)
Supports JavaScript, TypeScript, and Python
Falls back to lexical search when ast-grep unavailable

title: Ranked and token-bounded search results
story: As an iSDLC agent, I want search results ranked by relevance and bounded by a token budget so that I receive the most relevant results without overflowing my context window.
acceptance_criteria: Results ranked by relevance score
Agent can specify maximum token budget
Results exceeding budget truncated from lowest relevance
Duplicates removed across multiple search passes

title: Opt out of enhanced search
story: As an iSDLC user in a restricted environment, I want to opt out of enhanced search setup so that I can use the framework without installing additional tools.
acceptance_criteria: A --no-search-setup flag skips detection and installation
User can disable enhanced search after initial setup
Framework functions normally with Grep/Glob baseline
Opt-out decision is persisted in project configuration

title: Incremental agent migration to search abstraction
story: As an iSDLC developer, I want to migrate agents to the search abstraction incrementally so that I can validate the new approach on high-impact agents before committing to a full migration.
acceptance_criteria: Grep/Glob wrapped as baseline backend from day one
Non-migrated agents continue using Grep/Glob directly
High-impact agents migrated first (quick-scan, impact analysis)
Migration guide documents conversion process

title: Semantic code search with natural language queries
story: As an iSDLC agent, I want to search for code using natural language queries (e.g., 'where do we handle authentication') so that I can find relevant code even when specific keywords are not present.
acceptance_criteria: Local embedding model supported as default
Optional cloud embedding providers configurable
Natural language queries return ranked results
Index built locally and persisted between sessions

title: Sub-second indexed search for large codebases
story: As an iSDLC user with a 500K file codebase, I want indexed search that returns results in sub-second time so that analysis workflows complete in reasonable time.
acceptance_criteria: Trigram index built during setup or first search
Index incrementally updated on file changes
Sub-second query execution on 500K file codebases
Resource-aware index storage
